resources.string:
:
  Range blocking for database numbers.

This regex must end with dots, in this regex mode, these trailing dots represent the variable digits.

For example:
  .{5,}.. requires the prefix to be at least 5 digits long, while allowing a 2-digit variation at the end.

Matches database numbers by prefix, the prefix must be at least 5 digits long, while allowing a 2-digit variation at the end.

For example:
  If 1234567800 exists in database, a call from 1234567899 will also be blocked.

Check SMS content using remote large language models.

This is provided solely for demonstration purposes.

Tap to open the wiki page for instructions.
Register and log in to the website and get your token.
This setting is missing from the backup file and cannot be restored.

Whitelist and blacklist rules shouldn\'t have the same priority

Conflicting rules:

The maximum characters allowed in the input box. Exceeding this limit disables editing, as excessively long text will freeze the UI.

Default: 10000 characters
If the app crashes on startup and won\'t open, try clearing the history logs using the button below.

The server returns an explicit spam category, common in API services.

For example, the server returns:
  { …, \"type\": \"fraud\", … }
Match it with regex:
  \"type\": \"(fraud|scam|political)\"

Formula
Customize your own blocking logic based on the positive/negative rating counts returned by the server.

For example, block when:
  (negative > 5) & (negative / positive > 120%)

This regex is for extracting the positive rating count, a capturing group is required, meaning it should contain ( and ), defaults to 0 if no match is found.

For example:
  (\\d+) positive

This regex is for extracting the negative rating count, a capturing group is required, meaning it should contain ( and ), defaults to 0 if no match is found.

For example:
  (\\d+) negative
The number will be <highlight>allowed</highlight> if this condition is met.
The number will be <error>blocked</error> if this condition is met.

positive   p   negative   n
  Placeholders that will be replaced by the actual rating counts.
+   -   *   /
  Math basics (N/0=N)
%
  Percentage
<   >   <=   >=
  Comparisons
&   |
  Logical AND/OR
(   )
  Parentheses

Examples
  negative > positive
  P - N > 5 (case insensitive)
  p > 3 & n > 2 & (n / p >= 120%)
Regex missing capturing group (), check the tooltip for details.
